steps.title: Read the rhetorical question
steps.content: The passage repeatedly says the self is ultimately unknowable: "the soul of a man is unknowable" and "the final mystery is oneself." The underlined question asks who can calculate the orbit of his own soul, implying that no one can.

steps.title: Connect it to self-knowledge
steps.content: Choice B shifts to readers' doubts about value, not possibility. Choice C wrongly claims the text gives directions for self-knowledge, and choice D invents an unpopular definition. The rhetorical question reinforces the passage's skepticism about truly achieving self-knowledge. The answer is A.
